Passive Income

Money

I didn’t plan to start a business. I just wanted to automate a frustrating workflow that kept eating my evenings. A few JavaScript libraries, one weekend of coding, and countless iterations later, that side project evolved into a product people were happy to pay for every month.

5 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by orbtal media on Unsplash1. The Problem Wasn’t a Lack of Ideas — It Was Repeating the Same Work
Every freelance project seemed different.
Different client.
Different branding.
Different industry.
But underneath, the work was almost identical.
I found myself repeatedly building:

• Admin dashboards

• Authentication systems

• Payment pages

• Email notifications

• File uploads

• Analytics
At some point, I realized I wasn’t creating new software anymore.
